task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated clinical diagnosis. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist of the most relevant and methodologically rigorous papers.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML method applied directly to automated clinical diagnosis (disease detection, imaging diagnosis, or differential diagnosis from patient data). Prognosis, treatment selection, drug discovery, and methodology-only papers should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague method; 3=clear method with metrics; 4=full method with baselines and ablations.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy Diagnosis from Cardiac MRI
papers.abstract: We train a 3D convolutional network on 5,200 cardiac MRI cine stacks from the UK Biobank and three clinical centres to diagnose hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and distinguish it from athlete's heart and hypertensive cardiomyopathy. The model achieves AUC 0.97 for HCM detection and macro-AUC 0.93 for the three-way differential, outperforming CMR reporting cardiologists. Full ablation of temporal and structural features is provided.
papers.topic_hint: 3D CNN, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, cardiac MRI, UK Biobank, CMR diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: 3D CNN, 5200 CMR stacks, AUC 0.97, macro-AUC 0.93, cardiologist comparison, temporal/structural ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: 3D CNN for HCM differential diagnosis from CMR with AUC 0.97, outperforming CMR cardiologists.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Cardiovascular Drug Titration
papers.abstract: Machine learning for cardiac clinical decision support extends to treatment management. We apply deep RL to titrate antihypertensive medication in 2,100 hypertension patients using outpatient blood pressure readings, achieving target control in 71% of patients versus 54% for guideline-based titration. This is a treatment selection system, not a diagnostic tool.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, antihypertensive, drug titration, hypertension management
papers.methodology_hint: deep RL, antihypertensive titration, 2100 patients, 71% target control, treatment not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: RL antihypertensive titration achieving 71% target control versus 54% guideline-based titration.

papers.title: Transformer for Multi-Label Arrhythmia Classification from Holter ECG Recordings
papers.abstract: We develop a transformer trained on 75,000 48-hour Holter ECG recordings from four hospital systems to classify 20 arrhythmia classes simultaneously using multi-label learning. The model achieves macro-AUC of 0.94 and is validated prospectively in a blinded cardiologist reader study. Per-class ablation of the attention mechanism's temporal receptive field is reported alongside public code release.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, Holter ECG, arrhythmia, multi-label, 20-class, cardiac diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 75k 48-hour Holters, macro-AUC 0.94, prospective reader study, attention ablation, public code
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for 20-class Holter arrhythmia with macro-AUC 0.94, prospective validation and attention ablation.

papers.title: Bayesian Neural Network for 10-Year MACE Prediction in Stable Coronary Disease
papers.abstract: Machine learning for cardiovascular clinical decision support includes risk stratification. We train a Bayesian neural network on 28,000 stable coronary artery disease patients from the PROMISE trial to predict 10-year major adverse cardiovascular events, achieving C-index 0.79 with calibrated uncertainty. This is a prognostic tool informing statin and aspirin prescribing; no diagnostic classification is performed.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian NN, MACE prediction, coronary disease, PROMISE, prognosis, risk stratification
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian NN, PROMISE trial 28k patients, C-index 0.79, calibrated uncertainty, prognosis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian NN for 10-year MACE prognosis in coronary disease with C-index 0.79 and uncertainty calibration.

papers.title: Vision Transformer for Echocardiographic Valvular Disease Classification
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a ViT-B/16 on 42,000 echocardiographic videos to classify six valvular heart disease states including aortic stenosis, mitral regurgitation, and tricuspid regurgitation across severity grades. The model achieves macro-AUC 0.95 and sensitivity 0.90 at 85% specificity, validated against a board-certified echocardiographer consensus. Temporal attention maps highlight peak systole and diastole as the most informative frames.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, echocardiography, valvular heart disease, aortic stenosis, mitral regurgitation, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-B/16, 42k echo videos, macro-AUC 0.95, echocardiographer consensus, temporal attention maps
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for six-class echocardiographic valvular disease with macro-AUC 0.95 and temporal attention analysis.

papers.title: Molecular Dynamics Simulations for Cardiac Ion Channel Drug Binding
papers.abstract: We perform microsecond-scale molecular dynamics simulations of hERG potassium channel conformations to characterise drug binding poses for cardiotoxicity screening. Results identify three novel binding modes that explain class-specific QT prolongation. No ML diagnostic model is developed; this is computational structural biology.
papers.topic_hint: molecular dynamics, hERG, cardiotoxicity, ion channel, structural biology
papers.methodology_hint: molecular dynamics, hERG simulation, binding pose analysis, structural biology, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Microsecond MD simulations identifying three novel hERG binding modes explaining QT prolongation classes.

papers.title: GAN-Based Synthetic ECG Generation for Imbalanced Arrhythmia Training Data
papers.abstract: We train a conditional Wasserstein GAN on 12-lead ECG recordings to generate synthetic rare arrhythmia examples for training data augmentation. Classifiers trained with GAN-augmented data improve F1 by 9-15% for four rare arrhythmia classes on a held-out test set. This is a data augmentation methodology paper; no new diagnostic model is proposed.
papers.topic_hint: WGAN, synthetic ECG, data augmentation, arrhythmia, class imbalance
papers.methodology_hint: conditional WGAN, ECG augmentation, F1 improvement 9-15%, four rare arrhythmias, augmentation methodology
papers.claimed_contribution: Conditional WGAN for synthetic ECG augmentation improving rare arrhythmia F1 by 9-15%.

papers.title: Semi-Supervised Learning for Cardiac MRI Segmentation with Limited Labels
papers.abstract: We apply mean teacher semi-supervised training to cardiac cine MRI using 150 labelled and 3,000 unlabelled scans for left ventricular segmentation and ejection fraction estimation. Dice coefficient 0.93 and EF mean absolute error 3.1% are achieved. The method supports downstream functional analysis; no primary diagnosis is generated.
papers.topic_hint: semi-supervised learning, cardiac MRI, LV segmentation, ejection fraction
papers.methodology_hint: mean teacher, 150 labelled CMR, Dice 0.93, EF MAE 3.1%, functional analysis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Semi-supervised CMR segmentation with Dice 0.93 and EF MAE 3.1% using 150 labelled scans.

papers.title: ResNet-50 for Automated Detection of Structural Heart Disease from Chest X-Ray
papers.abstract: We train a ResNet-50 on 110,000 frontal chest X-rays to detect six structural cardiac abnormalities including cardiomegaly, pericardial effusion, and aortic root dilatation. The model achieves mean AUC of 0.92 across abnormalities, outperforming five radiology resident readers and a prior chest X-ray AI baseline. External validation on two independent health systems is reported.
papers.topic_hint: ResNet, chest X-ray, structural heart disease, cardiomegaly, cardiac diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50, 110k CXRs, mean AUC 0.92, 5 radiology resident comparison, two external validation cohorts
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet-50 for structural cardiac abnormality detection from CXR with mean AUC 0.92 and external validation.

papers.title: Causal Inference for Identifying Modifiable Risk Factors of Heart Failure
papers.abstract: We apply instrumental variable and doubly-robust methods to EHR data from 180,000 patients to identify causal risk factors for incident heart failure. Hypertension, obesity, and sleep apnoea are confirmed as causal; diabetes effect is attenuated after adjustment. No diagnostic model or prediction tool is developed.
papers.topic_hint: causal inference, heart failure, risk factors, instrumental variable, observational study
papers.methodology_hint: instrumental variables, doubly-robust, 180k patients, causal risk factor identification, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Causal identification of modifiable heart failure risk factors from 180,000-patient EHR study.

papers.title: Attention-Based RNN for Atrial Fibrillation Detection from PPG Waveforms
papers.abstract: We develop an attention-based recurrent neural network trained on 22,000 30-second PPG segments from wristband devices to detect paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. The model achieves AUC 0.96, sensitivity 0.94, and specificity 0.95, validated against 12-lead ECG gold standard. Attention weights correctly localise RR interval irregularity in inter-patient and intra-patient evaluations.
papers.topic_hint: RNN, attention, PPG, atrial fibrillation, wristband, cardiac diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: attention RNN, 22k PPG segments, AUC 0.96, sensitivity 0.94, ECG gold standard, attention localisation
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention RNN for AF detection from PPG wristband with AUC 0.96, validated against 12-lead ECG.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Distributed ECG Model Training Across Hospital Networks
papers.abstract: Machine learning for cardiac clinical decision support requires large ECG datasets distributed across hospitals. We apply federated learning to train an arrhythmia classification model across 20 hospital sites without sharing patient ECG data. The federated model achieves AUC 0.91, within 3% of centralised training. This is a federated training methodology; the diagnostic model uses a published architecture.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, ECG, arrhythmia, multi-site, training methodology
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, 20 hospital sites, AUC 0.91, 3% below centralised, methodology paper
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated ECG arrhythmia training across 20 sites achieving AUC 0.91, within 3% of centralised.

papers.title: XGBoost for Coronary Artery Disease Detection from Non-Invasive Biomarkers
papers.abstract: We apply XGBoost to a panel of 42 non-invasive biomarkers including cardiac troponin, BNP, CRP, and carotid intima-media thickness to detect obstructive coronary artery disease, defined as >50% stenosis on coronary angiography. In a prospective study of 2,400 patients, the model achieves AUC 0.88 and outperforms the Diamond-Forrester score and Duke Clinical Score baselines. Feature importance confirms troponin and IMT as dominant predictors.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, coronary artery disease, biomarkers, CAD detection, non-invasive diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost, 42 biomarkers, 2400 patients, AUC 0.88, Diamond-Forrester and Duke score baselines, feature importance
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost CAD detection from non-invasive biomarkers with AUC 0.88, outperforming Diamond-Forrester score.

papers.title: Survival Analysis for Heart Transplant Waitlist Mortality Prediction
papers.abstract: Machine learning for cardiac clinical decision support supports organ allocation decisions. We apply a neural network survival model to predict 90-day waitlist mortality for 4,800 heart transplant candidates using haemodynamic and laboratory features, achieving C-index 0.82 versus 0.74 for UNOS Status alone. Waitlist mortality prediction guides urgency listing; it does not diagnose cardiac disease.
papers.topic_hint: neural survival model, heart transplant, waitlist mortality, UNOS, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: neural survival model, 4800 transplant candidates, C-index 0.82, UNOS Status comparison, prognosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Neural survival model for heart transplant waitlist mortality with C-index 0.82 vs UNOS 0.74.

papers.title: CNN for Point-of-Care Echocardiography Quality Assessment and View Classification
papers.abstract: We develop a CNN trained on 45,000 echocardiographic clips to simultaneously assess image quality and classify standard views (apical 4-chamber, parasternal long axis, etc.). The model achieves view classification accuracy 0.94 and quality score correlation r=0.88 with expert rating. Real-time inference on a tablet platform is demonstrated. Note: view classification is a prerequisite for diagnosis, not a primary diagnostic output.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, echocardiography, view classification, quality assessment, point-of-care
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 45k echo clips, accuracy 0.94, correlation 0.88, real-time tablet deployment, view classification not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for point-of-care echo view classification and quality assessment with 0.94 accuracy in real-time.

papers.title: Interpretable Machine Learning for Cardiac Biomarker Threshold Derivation
papers.abstract: We use SHAP-based interpretable ML to derive data-driven thresholds for cardiac biomarkers (troponin, BNP, CRP) associated with adverse cardiac events in emergency presentations. Results challenge current guideline thresholds for high-sensitivity troponin. No predictive classification model is developed; this is a methodology paper for threshold analysis.
papers.topic_hint: SHAP, interpretable ML, cardiac biomarkers, troponin, threshold derivation, methodology
papers.methodology_hint: SHAP analysis, biomarker threshold derivation, guideline comparison, methodology paper, no classifier
papers.claimed_contribution: SHAP-based data-driven cardiac biomarker threshold derivation challenging current troponin guidelines.

papers.title: Deep Learning Radiomics for Coronary Plaque Characterisation from CCTA
papers.abstract: We extract radiomic features from coronary CT angiography and train a deep neural network to characterise plaque type as calcified, mixed, or non-calcified across 3,100 lesions. The model achieves AUC 0.90 for non-calcified plaque detection, outperforming manual radiomic feature extraction. Plaque characterisation supports risk stratification but is not a primary diagnosis of CAD presence.
papers.topic_hint: radiomics, deep learning, coronary CCTA, plaque characterisation, risk stratification
papers.methodology_hint: deep radiomics, CCTA, 3100 lesions, AUC 0.90, manual radiomic baseline, risk stratification not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep radiomics for CCTA coronary plaque characterisation with AUC 0.90 vs manual radiomic extraction.

papers.title: Transformer for Real-Time Cardiac Arrhythmia Detection from Implantable Device Streams
papers.abstract: We develop a lightweight transformer architecture for real-time arrhythmia detection from electrogram streams in implantable cardiac devices, operating within 8 KB memory and 2 ms inference time. Tested on 18,000 arrhythmia episodes from device logs, the model achieves sensitivity 0.96 and specificity 0.93 for ventricular tachycardia and fibrillation detection, matching device manufacturer algorithms while reducing inappropriate shocks by 22%.
papers.topic_hint: lightweight transformer, implantable device, arrhythmia detection, electrogram, real-time, VT/VF
papers.methodology_hint: lightweight transformer, 8KB, 18k device episodes, sensitivity 0.96, specificity 0.93, manufacturer baseline, shock reduction
papers.claimed_contribution: Lightweight transformer for implantable device VT/VF detection with sensitivity 0.96, reducing inappropriate shocks 22%.

papers.title: Meta-Analysis of Deep Learning for ECG Interpretation: Systematic Review
papers.abstract: We meta-analyse 112 studies on deep learning for ECG interpretation, covering arrhythmia detection, MI diagnosis, and channelopathy classification. Pooled AUC is 0.93 for binary tasks but drops to 0.82 for multi-class settings. We identify publication bias, lack of external validation, and class imbalance as primary methodological concerns. No new model is presented.
papers.topic_hint: meta-analysis, deep learning, ECG interpretation, arrhythmia, systematic review
papers.methodology_hint: meta-analysis, 112 studies, pooled AUC, publication bias analysis, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Meta-analysis of 112 deep learning ECG studies identifying publication bias and validation gaps.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Multi-Scale Cardiac Function Phenotyping from CMR
papers.abstract: We construct subject-specific cardiac mesh graphs from CMR imaging and train a GNN to predict six functional phenotypes including ejection fraction, strain, and diastolic parameters. Mean absolute error of 2.1% for EF and strong correlations for strain metrics are achieved versus manual analysis. Functional phenotyping from CMR supports research and clinical monitoring but does not perform diagnostic classification.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, cardiac mesh, CMR, functional phenotyping, ejection fraction, strain
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, cardiac mesh graphs, EF MAE 2.1%, strain correlation, functional phenotyping not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for multi-scale cardiac functional phenotyping from CMR meshes with EF MAE 2.1%.
